meta.description: Sync from akave repo tag v0.4.4: remove PolicyFactory contract, rename proto package nodeapi→ipcnodeapi, refactor error sentinel variables, add connection pooling to SDK/IPC structs, add Block() test helper

execution_order: CHANGE-1: Remove PolicyFactory contract — apply first, it affects Config, Client struct, and deployment flow
CHANGE-2: Proto package rename (nodeapi → ipcnodeapi) — apply second if any code still references the old name
CHANGE-3: SDK/IPC struct refactoring (remove legacy client field, add connection pool) — apply third after dependent contract types are settled
CHANGE-4: Error sentinel variable refactoring — apply fourth, no wire-level change but improves type-safe error matching
CHANGE-5: Add Block() random-data test helper — apply last, test-only utility

changes.title: Remove PolicyFactory contract and its references
changes.description: The PolicyFactory smart contract and all its Go bindings were removed. The `ipc.Client` struct lost three fields (`PolicyFactory`, `ListPolicyAbi`, `PolicyFactoryAbi`). The `ipc.Config` struct lost `PolicyFactoryContractAddress`. The `ipc.ContractsAddresses` struct lost the `PolicyFactory` field. The public `DeployListPolicy` method was replaced by `TestDeployListPolicy` which deploys `ListPolicy` directly (no factory). `DeployContracts` no longer deploys `ListPolicy` or `PolicyFactory`.
changes.current_state:
The Rust SDK (`src/blockchain/provider.rs`) does NOT have a PolicyFactory struct or contract binding. The `BlockchainProvider` struct contains only:
```rust
pub struct BlockchainProvider {
    pub web3_provider: Web3<ProviderType>,
    confirmations: usize,
    #[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))]
    key: Option<SecretKey>,
}
```
There is no `policy_factory.rs`, `policy_factory.json`, or any PolicyFactory reference in `src/blockchain/`. The Rust SDK never implemented this contract.
changes.required_change: No code changes are required in the Rust SDK — the Rust SDK never implemented PolicyFactory. However, if any initialisation code or documentation in `src/sdk.rs` references a `policy_factory_address` configuration field or contract address, that field and all references must be deleted. Verify by searching the entire repo for any of: `policy_factory`, `PolicyFactory`, `policyFactory`. Remove any that are found.

changes.title: Proto package rename: nodeapi → ipcnodeapi
changes.description: The gRPC protobuf files were renamed from `nodeapi.*` to `ipcnodeapi.*`. The proto package name changed from `nodeapi` to `ipcnodeapi`. The generated Go code and service name for the gRPC service is now under the `ipcnodeapi` package. The Go package import path stays `akave.ai/akave/private/pb`. The service interfaces are identical in structure — only the proto file name and package name changed.
changes.current_state:
The Rust SDK already has `proto/ipcnodeapi.proto` (SHA: 5d4aea25e829b22604db347ce050d617af899016). Its content matches the new Go proto content exactly in terms of services and messages:
- Service: `IPCNodeAPI` with RPCs: ConnectionParams, BucketView, BucketList, FileUploadChunkCreate, FileUploadBlock, FileView, FileDownloadCreate, FileDownloadRangeCreate, FileDownloadChunkCreate, FileDownloadBlock, FileList, FileUploadBlockUnary
- Service: `IPCArchivalAPI` with RPC: FileResolveBlock
- All messages: ConnectionParamsRequest/Response, IPCBucket*, IPCFile*, IPCChunk, etc.
The Rust proto is already at the correct version.
changes.required_change: No changes required. The Rust SDK's `proto/ipcnodeapi.proto` is already using the correct name and content. If `build.rs` or any Rust source file still refers to `nodeapi` as a proto file or generated module name, rename those references to `ipcnodeapi`. Search the repo for `nodeapi` to confirm there are no stale references.

changes.title: SDK and IPC struct refactoring: remove legacy client field, add connection pool
changes.description: The top-level `SDK` struct had a `client pb.NodeAPIClient` field that is no longer initialised in `New()` — it was a direct gRPC client reference that is now obsolete because `IPC()` creates its own `IPCNodeAPIClient` on demand. A new `pool *connectionPool` field was added to both the `SDK` struct and the `IPC` struct to manage connection multiplexing. The `IPC` struct lost `conn *grpc.ClientConn` (no longer stores the raw connection). `SDK.Close()` now calls `errors.Join(sdk.conn.Close(), sdk.pool.close())` instead of only `sdk.conn.Close()`.
changes.current_state: Unknown — the Rust `src/sdk.rs` file is 126 KB and was not fully read. The implementing agent must read it and identify the equivalent of `pb.NodeAPIClient` (any direct gRPC client stored at SDK level that was created at construction time) and any equivalent of `conn *grpc.ClientConn` stored inside the IPC context. Identify the `close`/`drop` implementation to see if it needs to release a connection pool.
changes.required_change:
1. In the Rust SDK top-level struct (equivalent to `SDK`), remove any field that holds a pre-constructed NodeAPI/gRPC client. The client should be constructed lazily inside the IPC initialisation function, not at SDK construction time.
2. Add or confirm the presence of a connection pool mechanism. If none exists, add a simple wrapper that caches IPC channel handles keyed by target address.
3. In the `IPC`-equivalent struct, remove the field that stores the raw connection handle (`conn *grpc.ClientConn` equivalent). Replace with a reference to the pool.
4. Update the `close`/`drop` implementation to close the connection pool in addition to the main connection.

changes.title: Error sentinel variable refactoring in ipc/errors.go
changes.description: All errors returned by `ErrorHashToError` were refactored from inline `errors.New("SomeName")` to named package-level sentinel variables (e.g., `ErrBucketAlreadyExists = errors.New("BucketAlreadyExists")`). The error hash codes are identical between old and new — no wire-level change. `IgnoreOffsetError` changed its comparison from string equality (`err.Error() == "OffsetOutOfBounds"`) to type-safe comparison (`errors.Is(err, ErrOffsetOutOfBounds)`). This enables callers to use `errors.Is()` for type-safe error matching instead of string comparison.
changes.required_change:
The Go error table contains 13 errors whose corresponding Solidity error signatures are absent from the Rust `contract_errors.rs`. Add the following entries to the `sol! { }` block and to the `match_selectors!` macro call in `decode_revert_reason`:

1. `error NoPolicy();`                          — selector hash: `0xcefa6b05`
2. `error NotBucketOwner();`                    — selector hash: `0x0d6b18f0`
3. `error BucketNotFound();`                    — selector hash: `0xc4c1a0c5`
4. `error FileDoesNotExist();`                  — selector hash: `0x3bcbb0de`
5. `error NotThePolicyOwner();`                 — selector hash: `0xa2c09fea`
6. `error CloneArgumentsTooLong();`             — selector hash: `0x94289054`
7. `error Create2EmptyBytecode();`              — selector hash: `0x4ca249dc`
8. `error AlreadyWhitelisted();`                — selector hash: `0xb73e95e1`
9. `error InvalidAddress();`                    — selector hash: `0xe6c4247b`
10. `error NotWhitelisted();`                   — selector hash: `0x584a7938`
11. `error MathOverflowedMulDiv();`             — selector hash: `0x227bc153`
12. `error NotSignedByBucketOwner();`           — selector hash: `0x48e0ed68`
13. `error NonceAlreadyUsed();`                 — selector hash: `0x923b8cbb`

Note: `FileNonexists` (selector `0x21584586`) is also in Go but does not appear in the Rust list — add `error FileNonexists();` as well if it is missing.

changes.title: Add Block() random-data block test helper to testrand
changes.description: A new test utility function `Block(t *testing.T, size memory.Size) blocks.Block` was added to the Go testrand package. It generates a random block of a given byte size and computes a CIDv1/SHA2-256/DagProtobuf content identifier for it. This is used to generate well-formed IPFS blocks for test input without needing a running node.
changes.current_state: Unknown — the Rust `tests/` directory was not fully read. The implementing agent should search for an equivalent test utility that generates a random IPFS block (CIDv1 + random bytes) in the Rust test support code.

changes.title: Makefile and README updates
changes.description: Build targets and documentation were updated for v0.4.4. The Makefile had 9 additions and 13 deletions. The README had 2 additions and 127 deletions (major pruning). These are Go-specific build system and documentation changes with no direct Rust SDK impact.
changes.current_state: Not applicable — Makefile and README.md are Go SDK artifacts.
changes.required_change: No changes required in the Rust SDK. However, if the Rust SDK's own README references the proto package name `nodeapi`, update those references to `ipcnodeapi`.

changes.title: Go dependency updates (go.mod/go.sum)
changes.description: Go module dependencies were updated for v0.4.4 compatibility (301 additions, 49 deletions in go.mod). This includes updated versions of ethereum, libp2p, ipfs, and other Go dependencies.
changes.current_state: The Rust SDK uses Cargo for dependency management (`Cargo.toml`). These are Go-specific dependency changes.
changes.required_change: No changes required unless the Rust SDK's Cargo.toml has specific version pins for ethereum/alloy/web3 crates that need to stay in sync with the Go SDK's go.mod. Review Cargo.toml for any crates that track Go library versions and update if needed.

changes.title: access_manager.go minor change
changes.description: The AccessManager contract binding had a trivial change (1 addition, 1 deletion — likely a formatting or comment update). No functions were added or removed. All 30 existing functions remain unchanged.
changes.current_state: The Rust SDK `src/blockchain/access_manager.rs` and `src/blockchain/access_manager.json` have full AccessManager contract bindings. No structural changes are expected.
changes.required_change: No changes required unless a function signature changed. Verify by comparing the `access_manager.json` ABI in this repo against the current Go contract ABI. If a function input/output type changed, update accordingly in `access_manager.json` and in any call sites in `access_manager.rs`.
changes.code_snippet_before: N/A — trivial change, exact diff not extracted
changes.code_snippet_after: N/A — trivial change, exact diff not extracted

contract_updates.encoding_notes: No type encoding changes. The new `setAuthority` function takes a single `address` parameter (20-byte Ethereum address). The Rust SDK's `src/blockchain/storage.json` already contains this function entry — no ABI file update is required. If a Rust binding method `set_authority(authority: Address)` is desired, add it to `src/blockchain/storage.rs` as a call to `call_contract_with_confirmations(&self.contract, "setAuthority", (authority,), None)`.
The PolicyFactory contract was removed from the Go SDK. The Rust SDK never implemented PolicyFactory, so no ABI file or binding changes are needed.

test_updates.description: Integration test that deploys all contracts (AkaveToken, Storage via ERC1967 proxy, AccessManager) and verifies the deployment. In v0.4.4 the test no longer deploys or references PolicyFactory. It now calls `TestDeployListPolicy` (the renamed direct deployment method) instead of the old `DeployListPolicy` method that used PolicyFactory.
test_updates.port_instructions:
1. In the Rust integration test equivalent of `TestContracts`, remove any steps that deploy a `PolicyFactory` contract or call a `deploy_list_policy` method that goes through PolicyFactory.
2. If a list policy deployment test is needed, use direct deployment: deploy a `ListPolicy` contract directly and call its `initialize(user_address)` method.
3. Ensure the test still verifies Storage, AkaveToken, and AccessManager deployment and their inter-wiring (minterRole grant, setAccessManager call).

test_updates.description: Integration test that exercises addFileChunks and fillChunkBlocks contract methods. This test was modified in v0.4.4 (8 changes: 8 additions, 8 deletions) but no new test cases were added.
test_updates.port_instructions: Review the equivalent Rust integration test for `add_file_chunks` and `fill_chunk_blocks`. No structural port changes are required unless the function signatures changed — verify against the updated Go test to confirm the method arguments match what the Rust SDK calls.
